title: Private Partnerships and Philanthropy Intern
compensation: Internship conditions apply under UNHCR policy
summary: UNHCR is recruiting an intern to support private partnerships and philanthropy work at headquarters in Copenhagen, with emphasis on coordination, events, communications and knowledge management.
description: The internship supports UNHCR's Private Partnerships and Philanthropy team in coordinating donor and partner engagement work. The intern will help with event planning, internal communications, knowledge management, SharePoint content, tracking tools, meeting preparation and post-event follow-up. The role is suited to candidates interested in humanitarian work, private sector engagement, communications, philanthropy and partnership coordination.
responsibilities: Support planning, coordination and delivery of events, meetings and engagement moments, especially during the December-January peak period.
Maintain workplans, timelines, trackers, participant lists and follow-up tools.
Prepare agendas, briefing notes, talking points, meeting materials and concise internal updates.
Support coordination across PPH workstreams and help consolidate inputs from colleagues.
Draft or update internal news, SharePoint content, knowledge products, templates and team materials.
requirements: Current student or recent graduate who meets UNHCR internship eligibility rules.
At least two years of undergraduate studies completed in a relevant field.
Studies or interest in international relations, communications, business administration, public policy, development studies, event management or knowledge management.
Strong written and verbal English, good attention to detail, and ability to work in a multicultural team.
Experience with events, communications, coordination, SharePoint or Microsoft Office tools is desirable.
benefits: Practical experience in humanitarian partnerships and private sector engagement.
Exposure to event coordination, knowledge management, internal communications and donor stewardship work.
Opportunity to work with a global humanitarian organization.
how_to_apply: Apply through the official UNHCR Workday vacancy page before the stated deadline.
verification_notes: Found through Google search and verified on the official UNHCR Workday vacancy page.

title: Unpaid Internship - HR Policy Intern
summary: ITU is recruiting an HR Policy Intern to support research, policy analysis, drafting and consultation work within the Human Resources Management Department.
description: The intern will work with ITU's Human Resources Management Department under the supervision of the Senior HR Policy Advisor. The assignment focuses on HR policy research, benchmarking, internal guidance, policy implementation monitoring and support for consultation and review processes. The internship may be carried out at ITU headquarters in Geneva or remotely, subject to ITU arrangements.
responsibilities: Conduct research for HR policies and projects using UN common system and public/private sector references.
Help prepare analytical documents, policy briefs, benchmarks and internal guidance materials.
Support monitoring of policy implementation and identify risks or possible mitigation measures.
Interact with HRMD and other ITU colleagues to gather relevant data and information.
Assist with consultation processes, review comments and support finalization of policies and projects.
requirements: Completed secondary education and enrollment in a university degree programme, preferably in law, human resources, international administration, international relations or a related field.
No previous work experience is required.
Advanced English is required; French is an asset.
Candidates must meet ITU internship eligibility rules, including age and citizenship requirements.
Analytical, drafting, teamwork, discretion and Microsoft Office skills are important.
benefits: Experience in HR policy work within a UN specialized agency.
Exposure to policy drafting, consultation and implementation processes.
Remote or Geneva-based internship possibility as stated by ITU.
how_to_apply: Apply through the official ITU careers vacancy page.
verification_notes: Found through Google search and verified on the official ITU careers page.

title: Bid and Frameworks Administrator
compensation: Competitive salary and contributory pension scheme
deadline_label: Rolling review / not specified on source page
summary: Oxford Policy Management is hiring a Bid and Frameworks Administrator to support international development bidding, framework tracking and tender administration in its New Delhi office.
description: The role sits in OPM's Business Development team and supports framework umbrella agreements, call-down opportunities, expressions of interest, tenders, project data and internal bid coordination. The position works across consulting practices and business functions to help teams meet client requirements and internal bidding processes.
responsibilities: Support OPM's framework portfolio and related call-down opportunities.
Monitor and disseminate bidding opportunities to consulting staff.
Maintain framework trackers, CRM records and bidding databases.
Provide guidance on framework rules, requirements and bid processes.
Support bid documentation, compliance documents, formatting, handover and coordination across legal, finance, HR and project management teams.
requirements: Degree-level education in a relevant subject is desirable.
Research skills and understanding of international development business are desirable.
Strong organization, administration, prioritization and attention to detail.
Advanced Microsoft Office skills, especially Word, Excel and PowerPoint.
High standard of spoken and written English.
Candidates must already have the right to work in India.
benefits: Learning resources and opportunities to work across functions.
Hybrid working model and generous annual leave.
Wellbeing resources, Employee Assistance Programme and UnMind access.
Competitive salary and pension benefits.
how_to_apply: Apply through the official Oxford Policy Management iCIMS careers page.
verification_notes: Found through Google search, with the same opportunity also shared on LinkedIn by an OPM staff member; official source is the OPM iCIMS page.

title: Bid Officer - Pakistan
compensation: Competitive salary and contributory pension scheme
deadline_label: Rolling review / not specified on source page
summary: Oxford Policy Management is hiring a Bid Officer in Islamabad to support bidding for international development clients and large priority opportunities.
description: The Bid Officer will support assigned bids from pre-positioning through contract negotiation. The role includes bid planning, market analysis, proposal administration, quality checks, tender documentation and coordination with consultants and internal teams.
responsibilities: Support planning, organization and monitoring of bidding processes from pre-positioning to contract negotiations.
Help implement OPM bidding processes and use bidding tools and resources consistently.
Review proposals and tender documents for quality assurance and compliance.
Support large or strategic bids, expressions of interest and tenders for donors such as FCDO, EU, World Bank, UN and ADB.
Maintain bid timetables, coordinate inputs and support project budget development.
requirements: Degree-level education in a relevant field is desirable.
Experience in bidding for international development clients is desirable.
Strong administration, organization, time management and attention to detail.
Excellent written and spoken English.
Advanced Microsoft Office skills.
Candidates must already have the right to work in Pakistan.
benefits: Hybrid working model.
Generous leave, wellbeing resources and professional learning opportunities.
Work on bids and tenders for international development clients.
how_to_apply: Apply through the official Oxford Policy Management iCIMS careers page.
verification_notes: Found through Google search and verified on the official OPM careers page.

title: HEDEX Engagement Manager
deadline_label: Open until filled / not specified by source
summary: Campus Compact is hiring a remote HEDEX Engagement Manager to support a higher education democracy exchange platform and build user engagement across civic learning networks.
description: The Engagement Manager will help manage and grow the Higher Education Democracy Exchange platform, a collaboration involving Campus Compact and higher education partners. The role focuses on stakeholder engagement, communications, platform growth, subscriber outreach, content distribution, social channels, newsletters and user engagement events.
responsibilities: Execute communications and engagement strategies for HEDEX users.
Build and maintain digital channels, including social media, email newsletters and content distribution.
Curate relevant field news and promote platform features.
Engage external partners and coordinate with Campus Compact and AAC&U staff.
Support events, innovation sprints and platform adoption activities.
requirements: Experience managing public-facing projects and multiple stakeholders.
Strong communication, project management and audience-building skills.
Ability to manage virtual or in-person event logistics.
Comfort working remotely with regular travel for staff gatherings and events.
Commitment to civic engagement and the public purposes of higher education.
benefits: Competitive benefits package.
Remote work with flexible environment.
Employer retirement contribution and generous paid time off.
Professional development and organizational breaks.
how_to_apply: Apply through the official Campus Compact Greenhouse job page.
verification_notes: Found through Google search of Greenhouse job pages and verified on the official Campus Compact posting.

title: Senior AI Platform Engineer
location: Boulder, Colorado or remote within the United States
compensation: Target hiring range US$175,000-215,000 plus annual bonus eligibility
deadline_label: Open until filled / not specified by source
summary: Campminder is hiring a Senior AI Platform Engineer to help build and enable AI capabilities for its camp management software business.
description: The role is for an experienced AI platform engineer who can work remotely within the United States or from Campminder's Boulder office. The organization serves summer camps through digital tools and is investing in AI capabilities for staff and customers.
responsibilities: Build and support AI platform capabilities for internal teams and customer-facing products.
Collaborate with engineering, product and business operations colleagues.
Contribute to reliable, scalable and secure platform design.
Participate in peer and cultural interviews as part of a multi-step recruitment process.
Support responsible use of AI across the organization.
requirements: Senior engineering experience relevant to AI platforms and production systems.
Ability to work remotely within the United States with reliable high-speed internet.
Collaboration skills across engineering, product and business operations.
Comfort working in a stable, profitable software company serving the camp industry.
benefits: Medical, dental and vision coverage with employer contributions.
HSA contribution for eligible plans.
Remote or hybrid work options.
Parental leave, flexible PTO, 401(k) match, wellness allowance and AI learning support.
how_to_apply: Apply through the official Campminder Greenhouse job page.
verification_notes: Found through Google search of Greenhouse job pages and verified on the official Campminder posting.

title: Community Engagement Coordinator, San Joaquin County, CA

responsibilities: Communicate complex information clearly to applicants and community members.
Conduct phone and email outreach to applicants.
Provide high-quality customer service and maintain confidentiality.
Support in-person activities throughout San Joaquin County.
Work with the Program Associate and project team on community engagement tasks.

Must be authorized to work in the United States.
Ability to travel locally for project activities.
Strong communication, confidentiality and applicant support skills.
benefits: Part-time role of up to 29 hours per week.
Hourly compensation stated by source.
Opportunity to support direct cash transfer and community engagement work.

title: Business Development Specialist
deadline_label: Open until filled / not specified by source
summary: Launchpad Technologies is hiring a remote Business Development Specialist to develop new business and manage customer relationships across Latin America-focused talent services.
description: The role combines outbound business development with account management. The specialist will identify prospective customers, run outreach across email, LinkedIn and phone, book discovery calls, qualify opportunities and maintain customer relationships for a remote-first technology and staffing company.
responsibilities: Develop outbound outreach strategies for companies interested in remote Latin American talent.
Research qualified prospects through LinkedIn, job boards and industry directories.
Create email, LinkedIn and phone outreach sequences that lead to conversations.
Book and qualify meetings for the CEO.
Track outreach activity, pipeline and conversion metrics in CRM.
Serve as a primary contact for existing customers and schedule regular check-ins.
requirements: Business development, sales or account management experience.
Strong English communication skills.
Ability to work remotely during 7:30 AM-4:30 PM PST.
Comfort with LinkedIn outreach, CRM use and prospect research.
Residence in Latin America is aligned with the role location.
benefits: 100% remote work.
Compensation in USD.
Training allowance, home-office hardware setup and PTO.
Opportunity to work with global teams and clients.

Communicate clearly with stakeholders and build positive relationships.

title: Consultancy for Financial Review
organization: Raoul Wallenberg Institute of Human Rights and Humanitarian Law
duration: Assignment duration to be confirmed in Terms of Reference
summary: RWI invites consultants or consulting teams to submit proposals for a financial management diagnostic, modernization review and implementation support consultancy.
description: The consultancy will help RWI review and strengthen financial management systems, governance arrangements, policies, procedures, controls, reporting capabilities and organizational capacity. The work also supports planning for a future financial management system and implementation of agreed reforms.
responsibilities: Conduct a financial management diagnostic and modernization review.
Assess governance, policies, controls, procedures and reporting capabilities.
Identify practical improvement opportunities and implementation priorities.
Support planning for financial management system selection and reform implementation.
Provide professional recommendations, training, coaching or capacity-building where relevant.
requirements: Relevant experience in financial management diagnostics and modernization.
Experience with NGOs, foundations, universities, research institutions, development organizations or comparable entities.
Knowledge of Swedish regulatory, governance and financial reporting requirements for foundations and charitable organizations.
Ability to submit a proposal in line with the Terms of Reference.
Overall value for money will be considered in evaluation.
benefits: Competitive procurement opportunity with quality and price evaluation.
Opportunity to support institutional financial management modernization.
Engagement with a human rights and humanitarian law institute.
how_to_apply: Submit a proposal through the application link on the official RWI notice before the deadline.
verification_notes: Found through UNjobs/Google search and verified on the official RWI notice.

title: FAO Regular Volunteer Programme for Africa
organization: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO)
summary: FAO is accepting expressions of interest for its Regular Volunteer Programme for Africa, with assignments of up to six months in various locations.
description: The programme invites eligible volunteers for FAO assignments in Africa. Selected candidates should have the appropriate residence or immigration status for the proposed country of assignment and must meet FAO standards of integrity and professional conduct.
responsibilities: Support FAO work in assigned country or regional activities.
Complete tasks specified for the volunteer assignment after selection.
Follow FAO values, integrity standards and conduct requirements.
Provide required documents and cooperate with FAO recruitment and screening processes.
requirements: Candidates should have appropriate residence or immigration status in the proposed assignment country.
Candidates with immediate family members employed by FAO under any contract type are not eligible.
Applicants must complete an online profile through the FAO recruitment portal.
A letter of motivation and academic qualifications should be attached.
Incomplete applications will not be considered.
benefits: Volunteer experience with FAO in Africa.
Exposure to food security, agriculture, rural development and related FAO programme areas.
Assignment duration of up to six months.
how_to_apply: Apply through the official FAO recruitment portal and submit the required profile and documents.
verification_notes: Found through Google search and verified on the official FAO jobs page.

title: South Asia Researcher - Remote
organization: Armed Conflict Location and Event Data Project (ACLED)
duration: Initial consultancy period from July 2026 to June 2027
deadline_label: Apply as soon as possible; external listing indicates July 23, 2026
summary: ACLED is recruiting a remote South Asia Researcher to collect, review and manage political violence and protest data across South Asia.
description: The researcher will support ACLED's conflict data work across South Asia, including Bangladesh, Pakistan, India and Afghanistan. The role involves desk research, media monitoring, coding events, data collection and quality review. It is an entry-level independent consultant opportunity requiring regular availability and reliable internet access.
responsibilities: Collect and code political violence and protest events from news, NGO, international organization and security reports.
Record incidents into structured data templates.
Review and manage data for quality, consistency and reliability.
Complete ACLED training exercises and meet required coding standards.
Work to deadlines with limited supervision.
requirements: University degree in international relations, political science, conflict studies, peacebuilding, international development or a related field.
Familiarity with Excel.
Fluency in English.
Bengali is highly preferred; Urdu, Pashto, Dari or Hindi are also useful.
Experience with desk research, data work, media monitoring or conflict analysis is desirable.
Reliable internet, email and Microsoft Office access.
benefits: Fully remote consultancy opportunity.
Hourly remuneration stated by source.
Training and experience in conflict event data collection and political violence research.
how_to_apply: Apply through the ACLED careers portal. Applicants should confirm the current deadline on the official portal before submitting.
verification_notes: Found via UNjobs, DevNetJobs, Himalayas and LinkedIn/social search results; source link points to ACLED's career portal.

title: (Assoc) Program Manager, Human Trafficking Research Initiative
location: United States, Colombia, Mexico or Peru
summary: Innovations for Poverty Action is hiring an Associate Program Manager for the Human Trafficking Research Initiative to manage research sub-awards, donor reporting, evidence synthesis and partner coordination.
description: The role supports IPA's Human Trafficking Research Initiative, an eight-year programme focused on evidence for reducing human trafficking and protecting victims and survivors. The manager will oversee active research sub-awards, coordinate donor reporting and financial tracking, support research dissemination, manage internal systems and represent the initiative in research and policy spaces.
responsibilities: Manage a portfolio of active research sub-awards, including agreements, amendments, deliverables and finances.
Lead quarterly donor reporting by coordinating inputs from grantees, IPA country offices and internal staff.
Maintain internal project management and CRM systems.
Synthesize and disseminate human trafficking research through reports, briefs, blogs and presentations.
Coordinate webinars, research convenings, stakeholder mapping and new project development.
requirements: Master's degree in a relevant social science field such as economics, public policy, political science or international development.
At least four years of experience in research, monitoring and evaluation, programme management or international development.
Experience managing complex multi-stakeholder projects, grants, budgets and reporting.
Familiarity with quantitative research methods is preferred.
Professional proficiency in French or Spanish is preferred.
Regular international travel of up to 15 percent may be required.
benefits: Opportunity to work on a major human trafficking evidence initiative.
Engagement with research teams, country offices, donors and policy partners.
Role may be based in the United States or selected IPA country offices.
how_to_apply: Apply through the official listing associated with Innovations for Poverty Action and confirm application instructions on IPA's careers channels.
verification_notes: Found through LinkedIn/social search and UNjobs search results; LinkedIn appears to host the detailed public posting.
source: Innovations for Poverty Action LinkedIn Jobs
